examples: They only offered me five dollars, which I wouldn’t do it for less than fifteen.

definitions.text: What, of those mentioned or implied.
examples: They couldn't decide which song to play.
You may go which way you please.
He once owned a painting of the house, which painting would later be stolen.
She only speaks Chinese, which language I can hardly follow.

definitions.text: What one or ones (of those mentioned or implied).
examples: By now, you must surely know which is which.

definitions.text: In a non-restrictive relative clause, referring to something previously mentioned. Referring to a preceding noun.
examples: I dropped a saucepan, which knocked over the eggs, which went all over the kitchen floor.
I found my camera, which I thought I'd lost, under the bed.
